mission.title: openfga: Excessive DNS Queries After Upgrading to 1.8.14
mission.description: Excessive DNS Queries After Upgrading to 1.8.14. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify openfga troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your openfga deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfga -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga -n openfga --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review openfga configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant openfga configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n openfga -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga
kubectl get configmap -n openfga -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=openfga
```
Hello OpenFGA team,

After upgrading to version 1.8.14, we experienced a significant spike in DNS queries from our OpenFGA deployment, which led to a production incident.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Excessive DNS Queries After Upgrading to 1.8.14
steps.description:
thank you for that detailed analysis!

I ran a similar test against version 1.8.14:
```
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_OPEN_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_IDLE_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_IDLE_TIME=600s
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_LIFETIME (not set)
```

I observed my connections being reused
```yaml
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_OPEN_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_IDLE_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_IDLE_TIME=600s
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_LIFETIME (not set)
```

steps.title: Upgrade openfga to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade openfga:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade openfga openfga/openfga --namespace openfga
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfga
helm list -n openfga
```

steps.title: Confirm Excessive DNS Queries After Upgrading to 1.8.14 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga -n openfga --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n openfga --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
thank you for that detailed analysis!

I ran a similar test against version 1.8.14:
```
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_OPEN_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_IDLE_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_IDLE_TIME=600s
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_LIFETIME (not set)
```

I observed my connections being reused appropriately and remaining open when idle for longer than a couple of minutes.
codeSnippets:
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_OPEN_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_MAX_IDLE_CONNS=20
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_IDLE_TIME (not set)
OPENFGA_DATASTORE_CONN_MAX_LIFETIME (not set)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with openfga installed or the issue environment reproducible.
